deep_snippets.text: ## Chapter 4 Cell Structure 4.1 Studying Cells 4.2 Prokaryotic Cells 4.3 Eukaryotic Cells 4.4 The Endomembrane System and Proteins 4.5 The Cytoskeleton 4.6 Connections between Cells and Cellular Activities Close your eyes and picture a brick wall. What is the wall's basic building block? It is a single brick. Like a brick wall, cells are the building blocks that make up your body. Your body has many kinds of cells, each specialized for a specific purpose. Just as we use a variety of materials to build a home, the human body is constructed from many cell types. For example, epithelial cells protect the body's surface and cover the organs and body cavities within. Bone cells help to support and protect the body. Immune system cells fight invading bacteria. Additionally, blood and blood cells carry nutrients and oxygen throughout the body while removing carbon dioxide. Each of these cell types plays a vital role during the body's growth, development, and day-to-day maintenance. In spite of their enormous variety, however, cells from all organisms—even ones as diverse as bacteria, onion, and human—share certain fundamental characteristics. 4.1 Studying Cells

## Table 4.1 4.5 • The Cytoskeleton Cell Component Function Present in Prokaryotes? Present in Animal Cells? Present in Plant Cells? Cytoskeleton Maintains cell’s shape, secures organelles in specific positions, allows cytoplasm and vesicles to move within cell, and enables unicellular organisms to move independently Yes Yes Yes Flagella Cellular locomotion Some Some No, except for some plant sperm cells Cilia Cellular locomotion, movement of particles along plasma membrane's extracellular surface, and filtration Some Some No ## Table 4.1 4.6 Connections between Cells and Cellular Activities

> **[Figure 780, Page 981]** A wide diagram or timeline (800×308px) related to: Animal, Speed, Shortfin, mako, shark, Domestic. This image appears on page 981 of the textbook. ![Figure 780 - A wide diagram or timeline (800×308px) related to: Animal, S](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0780_99630413.jpg) cell is too large, then diffusion is ineffective and the center of the cell does not receive adequate nutrients nor is it able to effectively dispel its waste. An important concept in understanding how efficient diffusion is as a means of transport is the surface to volume ratio. Recall that any three-dimensional object has a surface area and volume; the ratio of these two quantities is the surface-to-volume ratio. Consider a cell shaped like a perfect sphere: it has a surface area of 4πr2, and a volume of (4/3)πr3. The surface-to-volume ratio of a sphere is 3/r; as the cell gets bigger, its surface to volume ratio decreases, making diffusion less efficient. The larger the size of the sphere, or animal, the less surface area for diffusion it possesses. The solution to producing larger organisms is for them to become multicellular. Specialization occurs in complex organisms, allowing cells to become more efficient at doing fewer tasks. For example, circulatory systems bring nutrients and remove waste, while respiratory systems provide oxygen for the cells and remove carbon dioxide from them. Other organ systems have developed further specialization of cells and tissues and efficiently control body functions. Moreover, surface-to-volume ratio applies to other areas of animal dev

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 原核细胞与真核细胞

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物
- **学段**: 高中
- **年级**: 高中10年级
- **章节**: 细胞的分子与结构
- **知识点**: 原核细胞与真核细胞
- **课标版本**: 普通高中课程标准（2017年版2020年修订）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> （生物 10年级）原核细胞与真核细胞的内容要求。学生应理解原核细胞与真核细胞的基本概念，掌握基本方法，能运用所学知识解决简单的实际问题。

### 1.2 学业要求
> 学生能准确描述原核细胞与真核细胞的相关概念，能进行基本的运算和推理，能解决与原核细胞与真核细胞相关的实际问题，形成初步的生物思维和素养。

### 1.3 教学提示
> 教学时应注重从学生熟悉的生活情境入手，引导学生通过具体操作、观察、归纳等方式理解原核细胞与真核细胞的概念。注重知识的发生发展过程，帮助学生建立知识之间的联系。

---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解原核细胞与真核细胞的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解原核细胞与真核细胞的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法
- 学生能够运用生物学学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习生物学的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验生物学探究的乐趣，树立学好生物学的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受生物学与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 原核细胞与真核细胞的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构
- 原核细胞与真核细胞的基本内容和基本方法

### 3.2 教学难点

- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
原核细胞与真核细胞是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“描述原核细胞与真核细胞的最大区别是原核细胞没有由核膜包被的细胞核”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「原核细胞与真核细胞」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“描述原核细胞与真核细胞的最大区别是原核细胞没有由核膜包被的细胞核”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: ## Chapter 4 Cell Structure 4.1 Studying Cells 4.2 Prokaryotic Cells 4.3 Eukaryotic Cells 4.4 The Endomembrane System and Proteins 4.5 The Cytoskeleton 4.6 Connections between Cells and Cellular Activities Close your eyes and picture a brick wall. What is the wall's basic building block? It is a single brick. Like a brick wall, cells are the building blocks that make up your body. Your body has many kinds of cells, each specialized for a specific purpose. Just as we use a variety of materials to build a home, the human body is constructed from many cell types. For example, epithelial cells protect the body's surface and cover the organs and body cavities within. Bone cells help to support and protect the body. Immune system cells fight invading bacteria. Additionally, blood and blood cells carry nutrients and oxygen throughout the body while removing carbon dioxide. Each of these cell types plays a vital role during the body's growth, development, and day-to-day maintenance. In spite of their enormous variety, however, cells from all organisms—even ones as diverse as bacteria, onion, and human—share certain fundamental characteristics. 4.1 Studying Cells

## Table 4.1 4.5 • The Cytoskeleton Cell Component Function Present in Prokaryotes? Present in Animal Cells? Present in Plant Cells? Cytoskeleton Maintains cell’s shape, secures organelles in specific positions, allows cytoplasm and vesicles to move within cell, and enables unicellular organisms to move independently Yes Yes Yes Flagella Cellular locomotion Some Some No, except for some plant sperm cells Cilia Cellular locomotion, movement of particles along plasma membrane's extracellular surface, and filtration Some Some No ## Table 4.1 4.6 Connections between Cells and Cellular Activities

This image appears on page 981 of the textbook. ![Figure 780 - A wide diagram or timeline (800×308px) related to: Animal, S](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0780_99630413.jpg) cell is too large, then diffusion is ineffective and the center of the cell does not receive adequate nutrients nor is it able to effectively dispel its waste. An important concept in understanding how efficient diffusion is as a means of transport is the surface to volume ratio. Recall that any three-dimensional object has a surface area and volume; the ratio of these two quantities is the surface-to-volume ratio. Consider a cell shaped like a perfect sphere: it has a surface area of 4πr2, and a volume of (4/3)πr3. The surface-to-volume ratio of a sphere is 3/r; as the cell gets bigger, its surface to volume ratio decreases, making diffusion less efficient. The larger the size of the sphere, or animal, the less surface area for diffusion it possesses. The solution to producing larger organisms is for them to become multicellular. Specialization occurs in complex organisms, allowing cells to become more efficient at doing fewer tasks. For example, circulatory systems bring nutrients and remove waste, while respiratory systems provide oxygen for the cells and remove carbon dioxide from them. Other organ systems have developed further specialization of cells and tissues and efficiently control body functions. Moreover, surface-to-volume ratio applies to other areas of animal dev

When viewed together, these eight characteristics serve to define life. Order FIGURE 1.10 A toad represents a highly organized structure consisting of cells, tissues, organs, and organ systems. (credit: “Ivengo”/Wikimedia Commons) Organisms are highly organized, coordinated structures that consist of one or more cells. Even very simple, singlecelled organisms are remarkably complex: inside each cell, atoms form molecules. These in turn form cell organelles and other cellular inclusions. In multicellular organisms (Figure 1.10), similar cells form tissues. Tissues, in turn, collaborate to create organs (body structures with a distinct function). Organs work together to form organ systems. Sensitivity or Response to Stimuli FIGURE 1.11 The leaves of this sensitive plant (Mimosa pudica) will instantly droop and fold when touched. After a few minutes, the plant returns to normal. (credit: Alex Lomas) Organisms respond to diverse stimuli. For example, plants can bend toward a source of light, climb on fences and walls, or respond to touch (Figure 1.11). Even tiny bacteria can move toward or away from chemicals (a process called chemotaxis) or light (phototaxis). Movement toward a stimulus is a positive response, while movement away from a stimulus is a negative response.
Watch this video (https://openstax.org/l/movement_plants) to see how plants respond to a stimulus—from opening to light, to wrapping a tendril around a branch, to capturing prey. exercises.stem: 依据课标「原核细胞与真核细胞」：描述原核细胞与真核细胞的最大区别是原核细胞没有由核膜包被的细胞核 请简要阐述并举例。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：观察多种多样的细胞，说明这些细胞具有多种形态和功能，但同时又都具有相似的基本结构
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「原核细胞与真核细胞」相关的生命现象。
